skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem:
1/(x + 1) + 1/(x − 1) = 4/(x² − 1)

What value of x satisfies the equation above?
rationale: Choice D is correct. The common denominator is (x + 1)(x − 1) = x² − 1. The left side becomes [(x − 1) + (x + 1)]/(x² − 1) = 2x/(x² − 1). Setting equal to 4/(x² − 1) gives 2x = 4, so x = 2. (x = ±1 would be excluded as denominators vanish.)
